Alexanderson Properties, Eric Alexanderson, Frederic L. Schul Te, Individually And As Trustee Of The Schulte Family Trust Dated February 14, 2004, Janet L. Schul Te, Individually And As Trustee Of The Schulte Family Trust Dated February 14, 2004s Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel
Off Calendar Nov-10-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Kadin Blonski, Esq. (510.548.7474) of Beckman, Feller & Chang PC for Defendants/Moving Party.
No appearances.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Mr. Blonksi seeks clarification as to the deposition date. The Court modifiess the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS ALEXANDERSON PROPERTIES, ERIC ALEXANDERSON, FREDERIC SCHULTE and JANET SCHULTE's Motion To Compel:

Defendants' motion to compel is granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiff Cliff Fogle shall provide verified, code-compliant responses to Defendants' Form Interrogatories, Set One, on or before November 22, 2022. Plaintiff Cliff Fogle shall also appear for his deposition on or before November 23, 2022. Additionally, the Court notes that it does not have a courtesy copy of the moving papers. Parties are hereby reminded of Local Rules requiring emailing of courtesy copies for all discovery motions. The Court further notes that this motion improperly combined various motions with different procedural and substantive requirements and violates the filing fee requirements of Govt. C. section 70617(a)(f).

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order repeating the substantive portion of the court's ruling via email to williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
